name: Precision
description: How exact and consistent your measurements are

content.overview: Precision = how exact/consistent your measurements are. Like how many decimal places you can measure. A scale reading 65.347 kg is more precise than one reading 65 kg. {{viz:diagram-01}}
content.explanation:
Precision is about EXACTNESS and REPEATABILITY. Not about being right, about being exact.

Imagine throwing 3 darts:
- All 3 darts hit the same small spot (even if not at center). PRECISE.
- Darts scattered all over. NOT precise.

Precision = how closely grouped your measurements are. {{viz:anim-01}}

A scale showing 65.2347 kg is MORE precise than one showing 65 kg (more decimal places).

BUT WAIT! That precise scale might be WRONG (inaccurate). It could consistently read high by 5 kg. Precise but not accurate! {{viz:chart-01}}

Key idea: You can be precise without being accurate. Like a broken clock that's always 10 minutes fast—very consistent (precise) but always wrong (inaccurate).
keyPoints: Precision = exactness and consistency of measurements
A scale showing 65.347 is more precise than 65
You can be precise but not accurate!
Precision is about how finely you measure, not if you're right
content.whyItMatters: A precise measurement tells you the tool quality. It shows how carefully you can measure. But if it's wrong (inaccurate), precision doesn't help!
